---
title: "Pipeline Report on Transcriptome Assembly"
author: "Laboratory of Integrative Bioinformatics, Unidad de Genómica Avanzada, UChile | Bioinformatics Multidisciplinary Environment, UFRN"
date: today
params:
    counts_genes: "BambuOutput_counts_gene_validated.txt"
    counts_transcript: "BambuOutput_counts_transcript_validated.txt"
    fl_counts_transcript: "BambuOutput_fullLengthCounts_transcript_validated.txt"
    u_counts_transcript: "BambuOutput_uniqueCounts_transcript_validated.txt"
    transcriptome_meta: "annotated_transcriptome_metadata.csv"
    protein_coding_meta: "annotated_protein-coding_metadata.csv"
    lncrna_meta: "annotated_lncRNAs_metadata.csv"
    protein_coding_exonlength: "annotated_protein-coding_exonlength.csv"
    lncrna_exonlength: "annotated_lncRNAs_exonlength.csv"
    novel_lncrna_meta: "novel_pc_lnc_RNAs_metadata.csv"
    novel_lncrna_length: "novel_lncRNA_exon_lengths.csv"
    novel_protein_coding_length: "novel_protein-coding_exon_lengths.csv"
    plot_heatmap_gene: "heatmap_gene.png"
    plot_heatmap_tcpt: "heatmap_transcript.png"
    plot_pca_grouped: "pca_grouped.png"
    plot_pca: "pca.png"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-tools: true
    code-fold: true
    smooth-scroll: true
    theme: cosmo
    lightbox: true
    embed-resources: true
    df-print: kable
include-before-body:
  text: |
    <div style="background-color: black; width: 100%; display: flex; justify-content: center; margin-bottom: 20px;">
      <div style="display: flex; justify-content: center; padding: 10px 0;">
        <img src="logo/nf-core-longnoncoder_logo_dark2.png" width="300" height="100">
      </div>
    </div>
    <br>
---

# Environment setup

This first code chunk loads all the necessary R libraries for data manipulation and visualization.

```{r}
#| label: load-libraries
#| include: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(cowplot)
library(scales)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(viridis)
library(tidyr)
```

# Overall statistics

### Distribution of total numbers of annotated, novel, full-length and uniquely mapped transcripts assembled by Bambu

```{r}
#| label: get-overall-assembly-stats
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# List bambu outputs to account for assembled and quantified genes/transcripts
bambu_outputs <- c(
  genes = params$counts_genes,
  transcripts = params$counts_transcript,
  fullLength = params$fl_counts_transcript,
  uniquelyMapped = params$u_counts_transcript
)

# Check if files exist
files_exist <- sapply(bambu_outputs, file.exists)
if (!all(files_exist)) {
  missing_files <- bambu_outputs[!files_exist]
  stop("Error: The following files were not found: ", paste(missing_files, collapse = ", "))
}

# Store results in a list
results_list <- list()

for (name in names(bambu_outputs)) {
  current_file <- bambu_outputs[[name]]

  tryCatch({
    data <- read.delim(current_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

    # Get the first column, which contains the IDs
    id_column <- data[[1]]

    # Count total, annotated ("ENS..."), and novel ("Bambu...") entries
    total_count <- nrow(data)
    annotated_count <- sum(startsWith(id_column, "ENS"))
    novel_count <- sum(startsWith(id_column, "Bambu"))

    # Create the data frame with the new columns
    file_result <- data.frame(
      Bambu = name,
      Total = total_count,
      Annotated = annotated_count,
      Novel = novel_count
    )

    # Add this result to a list
    results_list[[name]] <- file_result

  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Warning: Could not process file '", basename(current_file), "' (named '", name, "'). Skipping.\n", sep = "")
    cat("Error message:", e$message, "\n")
  })
}

# Organize results in a single dataframe
if (length(results_list) > 0) {
  bambu_df <- do.call(rbind, results_list)

  # Reset the row names for a cleaner look
  rownames(bambu_df) <- NULL

  bambu_df <- bambu_df %>%
  # Create a new column to group by 'Gene' or 'Transcript'
  mutate(FeatureType = if_else(Bambu == "genes", "Gene", "Transcript")) %>%
  # Reshape data from wide to long format for faceting
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Total, Annotated, Novel),
    names_to = "Status",
    values_to = "Count"
  )

  # Save the results to a CSV file for later use
  write.csv(bambu_df, "Bambu_assembly_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)


  bambu_df

} else {
  cat("No files were successfully processed from the provided list.\n")
}

```

```{r}
#| label: prepare-to-plot-overall-assembly-stats

# Check if the required data frame exist
if (!exists("bambu_df")) {
  stop("Error: The data frame 'bambu_df' was not found. Please run the data processing script first.")
}

# Define the custom, more readable names for the plot axis
custom_labels <- c(
  "genes" = "Genes",
  "transcripts" = "All transcripts",
  "fullLength" = "Full length",
  "uniquelyMapped" = "Uniquely mapped"
)

# Set the order for the facets and the y-axis for a logical layout
bambu_df$Status <- factor(bambu_df$Status, levels = c("Total", "Annotated", "Novel"))
bambu_df$Bambu <- factor(bambu_df$Bambu, levels = rev(c("genes", "transcripts", "fullLength", "uniquelyMapped")))
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-bambu-gene-transcript-stats
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5

bambu_gene_data <- bambu_df %>% filter(FeatureType == "Gene")

bambu_gene_plot <- ggplot(bambu_gene_data, aes(x = Status, y = Count, fill = Status)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, position= "dodge") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), vjust = -0.3, size = 4) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  labs(
    title = "Genes",
    x = "\nStatus",
    y = "No. of genes\n"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

bambu_transcript_data <- bambu_df %>% filter(FeatureType == "Transcript")

# Apply the custom labels to the transcript categories
bambu_transcript_data$Bambu <- factor(bambu_transcript_data$Bambu, levels = names(custom_labels)[-1])
levels(bambu_transcript_data$Bambu) <- custom_labels[-1]

bambu_transcript_plot <- ggplot(bambu_transcript_data, aes(x = Bambu, y = Count, fill = Status)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Count),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 4
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Transcripts",
    x = "\nAssembly",
    y = "No. of transcripts\n",
    fill = "Status"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

bambu_plot <- plot_grid(bambu_gene_plot, bambu_transcript_plot, ncol = 2,
                        rel_widths = c(0.5, 1.5),
                        labels = "AUTO", label_size = 14, label_y = 0.85)
bambu_plot

ggsave("bambu_plot.png", bambu_plot, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 600, bg="white", units = "in")
```

### Bambu: Gene-level expression estimation and Spearman correlation between samples

```{r}
#| echo: false
knitr::include_graphics(params$plot_heatmap_gene)
```

### Bambu: Transcript-level expression estimation and Spearman correlation between samples

```{r}
#| echo: false
knitr::include_graphics(params$plot_heatmap_tcpt)
```

### Bambu: Principal Component Analysis

Separated by groupVar

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| out.width: "500px"
knitr::include_graphics(params$plot_pca_grouped)
```

Separated by individual samples

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| out.width: "500px"
knitr::include_graphics(params$plot_pca)
```

# Annotated RNAs Overview

## Introduction

This part of the scripts analyze and visualize the annotated transcriptome assembled and quantified by Bambu. We will explore the distribution of various gene and transcript biotypes, investigate transcript lengths, and compare exon characteristics between protein-coding and lncRNA genes.

## 1. Biotype Distribution Analysis

We'll begin by loading the transcriptome metadata and summarizing the counts of different transcript biotypes.

### Data Loading and Preparation

The `annotated_transcriptome_metadata.csv` file is loaded, and we count the occurrences of each transcript biotype, grouped by their corresponding gene biotype.

```{r}
#| label: load-and-prep-data

# Load transcriptome metadata
ann_txome <- read.csv(params$transcriptome_meta)

# Count each transcript biotype based on gene biotype
summary_data <- ann_txome %>%
  group_by(gene_biotype, transcript_biotype) %>%
  summarise(count = n_distinct(ensembl_transcript_id), .groups = 'drop')

# Calculate total transcripts to verify
total_transcripts_calculated <- sum(summary_data$count)
print(paste("Total transcripts calculated from summary_data:", total_transcripts_calculated))
```

### Categorization of Gene Biotypes

To make plotting more manageable, we categorize the gene biotypes into logical groups like protein-coding, lncRNAs, small RNAs, and others.

```{r}
#| label: categorize-biotypes

# --- Categorization of Gene Biotypes ---
protein_coding <- summary_data %>%
  filter(gene_biotype == "protein_coding")

lncRNAs <- summary_data %>%
  filter(gene_biotype == "lncRNA")

pseudogene_RNAs <- summary_data %>%
  filter(str_detect(gene_biotype, "pseudogene")) %>%
  mutate(category = "Pseudogene")

small_RNAs <- summary_data %>%
  filter(gene_biotype %in% c("snoRNA", "snRNA", "scaRNA", "scRNA", "sRNA", "rRNA", "misc_RNA", "miRNA"))

Tcell_RNAs <- summary_data %>%
  filter(gene_biotype %in% c("TR_V_gene", "TR_J_gene", "TR_D_gene", "TR_C_gene")) %>%
  mutate(category = "TCR elements")

IG_RNAs <- summary_data %>%
  filter(gene_biotype %in% c("IG_V_gene", "IG_J_gene", "IG_D_gene", "IG_C_gene")) %>%
  mutate(category = "Immunoglobulin elements")

other_RNAs_explicit <- summary_data %>%
  filter(gene_biotype %in% c("vault_RNA", "TEC", "ribozyme", "Mt_tRNA", "Mt_rRNA", "artifact")) %>%
  mutate(category = "Other")

# --- Combine 'Other' categories for a summary plot ---
all_other_RNAs <- bind_rows(pseudogene_RNAs, Tcell_RNAs, IG_RNAs, other_RNAs_explicit) %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(count = sum(count), .groups = 'drop')

all_other_RNAs$category <- factor(all_other_RNAs$category, levels = c("Pseudogene", "TCR elements", "Immunoglobulin elements", "Other"))
```

### Plotting Function

A reusable function is created to generate consistent bar plots for different data subsets. This function standardizes aesthetics, titles, and scales.

```{r}
#| label: define-plot-function

create_plot <- function(data, x, fill, title) {
  ggplot(data, aes(x = {{x}}, y = count, fill = {{fill}})) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge2") +
    labs(x = "\nGene biotypes", y = "Count (log10 scale)\n", fill = "Transcript biotypes",
         title = title) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size=12, hjust = 0.5),
          legend.position = "right",
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
          legend.text = element_text(size=12),
          plot.title = element_text(size=13, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
          axis.title.y = element_text(size=12, face = "bold"),
          axis.title.x = element_text(size= 12, face = "bold"),
          plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    scale_y_log10(
      breaks = trans_breaks("log10", function(x) 10^x),
      labels = comma
    ) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = count),
      position = position_dodge2(width = 0.9),
      vjust = -0.5,
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 4
    )
}
```

Here we generate and display the plots for each major RNA category.

```{r}
#| label: plot-biotypes

p1 <- create_plot(protein_coding, gene_biotype, transcript_biotype,
                  "Protein-coding RNAs") + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
                                                 axis.title.x = element_blank())
p2 <- create_plot(lncRNAs, gene_biotype, transcript_biotype, "LncRNAs") +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.title.x = element_blank())
p3 <- create_plot(small_RNAs, gene_biotype, transcript_biotype, "Small RNAs") +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.title.x = element_blank())
p4 <- create_plot(all_other_RNAs, category,  category, "All Other RNAs") +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.title.x = element_blank())

p5 <- create_plot(pseudogene_RNAs, gene_biotype, transcript_biotype, "Pseudogene RNA diversity")
p5 <- p5 + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.title.x = element_blank())
ggsave("pseudogene_RNAs_biotypes.png", p5, width = 15, height = 10, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")

p6 <- create_plot(other_RNAs_explicit, gene_biotype, transcript_biotype, "Other RNAs (detailed)") # Use other_RNAs_explicit here
p6 <- p6 + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.title.x = element_blank())

p7 <- create_plot(Tcell_RNAs, gene_biotype, transcript_biotype, "TCR element RNAs")
p7 <- p7 + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.title.x = element_blank())

p8 <- create_plot(IG_RNAs, gene_biotype, transcript_biotype, "Immunoglobulin element RNAs")
p8 <- p8 + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.title.x = element_blank())
```

#### Combined Panel of Biotype Plots

The individual plots are combined into a single panel for a comprehensive overview using `cowplot::plot_grid`.

```{r}
#| label: plot-panel-1
#| fig-width: 16
#| fig-height: 12

panel1 <- plot_grid(p1, p2, p3, p4, ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(1.3, 1),
                   labels = "AUTO", label_size = 14, label_y = 0.85)
panel1

ggsave("cowplot_panel_anntxome.png", panel1, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 600, bg="white", units = "in")
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-panel-2
#| fig-width: 20
#| fig-height: 12

# Create top row with p5 spanning full width
top_row_panel2 <- plot_grid(p5, ncol = 1, labels = "A", label_size = 14, label_y = 0.85)

# Create bottom row with the other three plots
bottom_row_panel2 <- plot_grid(p7, p8, p6,
                       ncol = 3,
                       labels = c("B", "C", "D"),
                       label_size = 14,
                       label_y = 0.85)

# Combine rows
panel2 <- plot_grid(top_row_panel2, bottom_row_panel2,
                   nrow = 2,
                   rel_heights = c(1, 1))

panel2

ggsave("cowplot_panel_anntxome_others.png", panel2,
       width = 20, height = 12, dpi = 600, bg = "white", units = "in")
```

#### Gene Biotype diversity across chromosomes

Here we generate and display the plots for each gene biotype across all assembled chromosomes.

Group, count, and prepare the chromosome data. Organize names, sort numbers and names and convert to factors to order them.

```{r}
#| label: summarize-chr-data

chr_gb <- ann_txome %>%
  mutate(
    gene_biotype = if_else(
      str_detect(gene_biotype, "pseudogene"),
      "pseudogene",
      gene_biotype
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(chromosome_name, gene_biotype) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Extract chromosome names
chromosome_names <- unique(chr_gb$chromosome_name)

# Separate numeric and non-numeric chromosomes
numeric_chromosomes <- grep("^[0-9]+$", chromosome_names, value = TRUE)
non_numeric_chromosomes <- setdiff(chromosome_names,
                                   numeric_chromosomes)

# Sort numeric chromosomes numerically
numeric_chromosomes_sorted <- as.character(sort(as.numeric(numeric_chromosomes)))

# Combine sorted numeric and non-numeric chromosomes
chromosome_order <- c(numeric_chromosomes_sorted, sort(non_numeric_chromosomes))

# Convert chromosome_name to a factor with the desired order
chr_gb$chromosome_name <- factor(chr_gb$chromosome_name, levels = chromosome_order)

head(chr_gb)
```

Plot

```{r}
#| label: plot-gb-across-chr
#| fig-width: 35
#| fig-height: 45

p9 <- ggplot(chr_gb, aes(x = gene_biotype, y = count)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           aes(fill = gene_biotype),
           alpha = 1,
           width = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ chromosome_name, scales = "free_x", ncol = 5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "viridis", guide = "none") +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "grey90"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5,
                               size = 20, margin = margin(t = 5, b = 10)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", color = "black",
                              margin = margin(t = 8, b = 8, l = 5, r = 5)),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey85", color = "grey70",
                                    linewidth = 0.5),
    text = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 32, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 24, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "cm")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Gene Biotype Diversity Across Human Chromosomes",
    subtitle = "Distribution of different gene types per chromosome (log10 scale)",
    x = "Gene Biotype",
    y = "Count (log10 scale)" )+
    geom_text(aes(label = count),
            vjust = -0.5,
            size = 5,
            color = "black")
p9

ggsave("gene_biotype_diversity_chr.png", p9,
       width = 35, height = 45, dpi = 300, bg = "white", units = "in")
ggsave("gene_biotype_diversity_chr.pdf", p9,
       width = 35, height = 45, bg = "white", units = "in")
```

## 2. Transcript Length Analysis

Next, we analyze the transcript lengths across different gene biotypes.

#### Length Distribution by Gene and Transcript Biotype

We filter for specific biotypes of interest and create boxplots to compare their transcript length distributions.

```{r}
#| label: plot-tx-length
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

selected_tx <- ann_txome %>%
  filter(gene_biotype %in% c("protein_coding", "lncRNA", "miRNA", "snoRNA", "snRNA"))

selected_tx$gene_biotype <- factor(selected_tx$gene_biotype,
                                   levels = c("protein_coding", "lncRNA", "miRNA", "snoRNA", "snRNA"))

median_stx <- selected_tx %>%
  group_by(gene_biotype) %>%
  summarise(median_length = median(transcript_length), .groups = 'drop')

p10 <- ggplot(selected_tx, aes(x = gene_biotype, y = transcript_length, fill = factor(gene_biotype))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Transcript length across gene biotypes",
       x = "Gene biotype",
       y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Gene biotype\n(median length)") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_stx$gene_biotype, " (", round(median_stx$median_length, 2), ")"))
```

We now look deeper into the `protein_coding` and `lncRNA` categories to see how transcript length varies by their respective *transcript* biotypes.

```{r}
#| label: plot-tx-length-detailed
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

# Protein-coding plot
median_pc <- selected_tx %>%
  filter(gene_biotype == "protein_coding") %>%
  group_by(transcript_biotype) %>%
  summarise(median_length_txb = median(transcript_length), .groups = 'drop')

p11 <- selected_tx %>%
  filter(gene_biotype == "protein_coding") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = gene_biotype, y = transcript_length, fill = transcript_biotype)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Transcript length across protein-coding biotypes",
       x = "Gene biotype", y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Transcript biotypes\n(median length)") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_pc$transcript_biotype, " (", round(median_pc$median_length_txb, 2), ")"))

# lncRNA plot
median_lnc <- selected_tx %>%
  filter(gene_biotype == "lncRNA") %>%
  group_by(transcript_biotype) %>%
  summarise(median_length_txb = median(transcript_length), .groups = 'drop')

p12 <- selected_tx %>%
  filter(gene_biotype == "lncRNA") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = gene_biotype, y = transcript_length, fill = transcript_biotype)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Transcript length across lncRNA biotypes",
       x = "Gene biotype", y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Transcript biotypes\n(median length)") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_lnc$transcript_biotype, " (", round(median_lnc$median_length_txb, 2), ")"))
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-panel3
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 10

panel3 <- plot_grid(p10, p11, p12,nrow = 3,
                    labels="AUTO",
                    label_size = 14,
                    label_y = 1,
                    rel_widths = c(1.5,2,1.5))

ggsave("cowplot_panel_txlen_diversity.png", panel3, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 600, bg="white", units = "in")

panel3
```

## 3. Exon Structure Analysis

Finally, we compare the exon number and length between protein-coding and lncRNA genes to see how these features contribute to final transcript length.

#### Data Loading for Exon Analysis

We load dedicated datasets for protein-coding and lncRNA genes and merge them.

```{r}
#| label: load-exon-data

# Load data
ann_pc_txome <- read.csv(params$protein_coding_meta)
ann_lnc_txome <- read.csv(params$lncrna_meta)

# Merge info
ann_pc_lnc_txome <- rbind(ann_pc_txome, ann_lnc_txome)
```

#### Exon Number Comparison

Here, we compare the number of exons per transcript for protein-coding genes versus lncRNAs.

```{r}
#| label: plot-exon-number
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

median_exnum <- ann_pc_lnc_txome %>%
  group_by(gene_biotype) %>%
  summarise(median_exnum = median(num_exons), .groups = 'drop')

p13 <- ggplot(ann_pc_lnc_txome, aes(x = gene_biotype, y = num_exons, fill = gene_biotype)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Number of exons across protein-coding and lncRNA biotypes",
       x = "Gene biotype", y = "Exon number (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Gene biotype\n(median number)") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_exnum$gene_biotype, " (", round(median_exnum$median_exnum, 2), ")"))

ggsave("exnum_gbiotypes.png", p13, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

Calculate median exon number across specific transcript biotypes

```{r}
#| label: plot-exon-number-median
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

median_pc_exnum <- ann_pc_txome %>%
  group_by(transcript_biotype) %>%
  summarise(median_exnum = median(num_exons), .groups = 'drop')

median_lnc_exnum <- ann_lnc_txome %>%
  group_by(transcript_biotype) %>%
  summarise(median_exnum = median(num_exons), .groups = 'drop')

p14 <- ggplot(ann_pc_txome, aes(x = gene_biotype, y = num_exons, fill = transcript_biotype)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")) +
  labs(title = "Number of exons across protein-coding transcript biotypes",
       x = "Gene biotype",
       y = "Exon number (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Transcript biotype\n(total median number)") +  # Set legend title
  scale_y_log10() +  # Add log10 scale to y-axis
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_pc_exnum$transcript_biotype, " (", round(median_pc_exnum$median_exnum, 2), ")"))  # Set legend labels

ggsave("exnum_pc_tbiotypes.png", p14, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")

p15 <- ggplot(ann_lnc_txome, aes(x = gene_biotype, y = num_exons, fill = transcript_biotype)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")) +
  labs(title = "Number of exons across lncRNA transcript biotypes",
       x = "Gene biotype",
       y = "Exon number (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Transcript biotype\n(total median number)") +  # Set legend title
  scale_y_log10() +  # Add log10 scale to y-axis
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_lnc_exnum$transcript_biotype, " (", round(median_lnc_exnum$median_exnum, 2), ")"))  # Set legend labels

ggsave("exnum_lnc_tbiotypes.png", p15, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-panel4
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 12

panel4 <- plot_grid(p13, p14, p15,nrow = 3,
                    labels="AUTO",
                    label_size = 14,
                    label_y = 1,
                    rel_widths = c(1.5,2,1.5))

ggsave("cowplot_panel_exnum_diversity.png", panel4, width = 6, height = 12, dpi = 600, bg="white", units = "in")

panel4
```

#### Mono- vs. Multi-exonic Transcript Analysis

We classify transcripts as "mono-exonic" (1 exon) or "multi-exonic" (\>1 exon) and investigate how this structural difference affects transcript counts and final transcript length.

```{r}
#| label: plot-mono-multi-exon
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 10

# Classify transcripts
ann_pc_lnc_txome$class_exon <- ifelse(ann_pc_lnc_txome$num_exons == 1, "mono-exonic", "multi-exonic")

# Count mono- and multi-exonic transcripts
count_momu <- ann_pc_lnc_txome %>%
  group_by(gene_biotype, transcript_biotype, class_exon) %>%
  summarise(count = n_distinct(ensembl_transcript_id), .groups = 'drop')

p16 <- ggplot(count_momu, aes(x = transcript_biotype, y = count, fill = transcript_biotype)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_grid(gene_biotype ~ class_exon, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of mono and multi-exonic transcripts",
       x = "Transcript biotype", y = "Count (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Transcript biotype") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  geom_text(aes(label = count), vjust = -0.5, size=3)

p16

ggsave("momu_tbiotypes.png", p16, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-mono-multi-exon-2
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 8

# mono and multi exon strctures affect transcript final length

# across tx biotypes

p17 <- ggplot(ann_pc_lnc_txome, aes(x = transcript_biotype, y = transcript_length, fill = transcript_biotype)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_grid(gene_biotype ~ class_exon, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title = "Length of mono and multi-exonic transcripts",
    x = "Transcript biotype",
    y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
    fill = "Transcript biotype"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
  ) +
  scale_y_log10()
p17

ggsave("momu_length_tbiotypes.png", p17, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-mono-multi-exon-3
#| fig-width: 5
#| fig-height: 5

# Plot length of mono vs multi-exonic transcripts
p18 <- ggplot(ann_pc_lnc_txome, aes(x = gene_biotype, y = transcript_length, fill = class_exon)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Mono and multi-exonic transcript length",
       x = "Gene biotype", y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Exon class") +
  scale_y_log10()

ggsave("momu_length_gbiotypes.png", p18, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")

p18
```

### Exon Number vs. Transcript Length Relationship

A 2D density plot helps visualize the relationship between the number of exons in a transcript and its total length for both protein-coding and lncRNA genes.

```{r}
#| label: plot-density
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

p19 <- ggplot(ann_pc_lnc_txome, aes(x = num_exons, y = transcript_length)) +
  stat_density2d(aes(fill = after_stat(level)), geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +
  facet_wrap(~gene_biotype, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(x = "Number of exons (log10 scale)",
       y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
       title = "Relationship between number of exons and transcript length")

p19

ggsave("exnum_tlen_gbiotypes.png", p19, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

### Individual exon length comparison

Now we gather exon length metadata!

```{r}
ann_pc_exon <- read.csv(params$protein_coding_exonlength)
ann_pc_exon$gene_biotype <- "protein_coding"

ann_lnc_exon <- read.csv(params$lncrna_exonlength)
ann_lnc_exon$gene_biotype <- "lncRNA"

ann_pc_lnc_exon <- rbind(ann_pc_exon, ann_lnc_exon)

ann_pc_lnc_exon <- merge(
  ann_pc_lnc_exon,
  ann_pc_lnc_txome[, c("ensembl_transcript_id", "class_exon")],
  by = "ensembl_transcript_id",
  all.x = TRUE
)
```

Plot

```{r}
#| label: plot-indiv-exlen
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

p20 <- ggplot(ann_pc_lnc_exon, aes(x = gene_biotype, y = width,
                                   fill = class_exon)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")) +
  labs(title = "Mono and multi-exon individual length across protein-coding\nand lncRNA gene biotypes",
       x = "Gene biotype",
       y = "Exon length (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Exon class") +  # Set legend title
  scale_y_log10() # Add log10 scale to y-axis

p20

ggsave("exlen_momu_gbiotypes.png", p20, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

# Novel protein-coding and lncRNAs Overview

## Introduction

This part of the scripts analyze and visualize the novel protein-coding and lncRNAs transcriptome assembled and quantified by Bambu. We will explore the distribution of various transcript sub-biotypes, their relationship with the reference annotation, transcript lengths, and compare exon characteristics between protein-coding and lncRNA genes. To do that, we capture the metadatas generated by aggregating multiple results from the tools Bambu, RNAmining and GFFCompare.

## 1. Biotype distribution analysis

We'll begin by loading the transcriptome metadata and summarizing the counts of different transcript biotypes.

### Data Loading and Preparation

The `novel_pc_lnc_RNAs_metadata.csv` file is loaded, and we count the occurrences of each biotype and their GFFCompare classifications.

```{r}
#| label: load-data-novel

novel_tx <- read.csv(params$novel_lncrna_meta)

novel_tx$biotype[novel_tx$prediction == "coding"] <- "novel protein_coding"
novel_tx$biotype[novel_tx$prediction == "non-coding"] <- "novel lncRNA"

head(novel_tx)
```

```{r}
#| label: summarize_data_1

novel_biotype_counts <- novel_tx %>%
  group_by(biotype) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')


novel_biotype_counts
```

```{r}
#| label: summarize_data_2

class_counts <- novel_tx %>%
  group_by(biotype, classification) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')


class_counts
```

Plotting

```{r}
#| label: plot-novel-biotypes-and-classes
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6

p21 <- ggplot(class_counts, aes(x = classification, y = count,
                         fill = classification)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(aes(label = count, y = count),
            vjust = -0.5,
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
            angle = 0,
            size = 3) +
  facet_grid(~ biotype, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  labs(title = "GFFCompare classification stats overview of novel protein-coding and lncRNAs",
       x = "\n\nClassification",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Classification") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.3, hjust =1),
        legend.position = "none") +
  scale_y_log10()
p21

ggsave("gffcompare_classes.png", p21, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

**Novel transcript biotype diversity across chromosomes**

Group, count, and prepare the chromosome data. Organize names, sort numbers and names and convert to factors to order them.

```{r}
#| label: summarize-chr-novel-data

chr_tb_novel <- novel_tx %>%
  group_by(seqnames, biotype) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Extract chromosome names
chromosome_names_novel <- unique(chr_tb_novel$seqnames)

# Separate numeric and non-numeric chromosomes
numeric_chromosomes_novel <- grep("^[0-9]+$", chromosome_names_novel, value = TRUE)
non_numeric_chromosomes_novel <- setdiff(chromosome_names_novel, numeric_chromosomes_novel)

# Sort numeric chromosomes numerically
numeric_chromosomes_sorted_novel <- as.character(sort(as.numeric(numeric_chromosomes_novel)))

# Combine sorted numeric and non-numeric chromosomes
chromosome_order_novel <- c(numeric_chromosomes_sorted_novel,
                      sort(non_numeric_chromosomes_novel))

# Convert chromosome_name to a factor with the desired order
chr_tb_novel$seqnames <- factor(chr_tb_novel$seqnames, levels = chromosome_order_novel)
head(chr_tb_novel)

chr_tb_novel_pc <- chr_tb_novel %>%
  filter(biotype == "novel protein_coding")

chr_tb_novel_lnc <- chr_tb_novel %>%
  filter(biotype == "novel lncRNA")
```

Plot

```{r}
#| label: plot-novel-across-chr
#| fig-width: 20
#| fig-height: 40

p22 <- ggplot(chr_tb_novel, aes(x = biotype, y = count)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           aes(fill = biotype),
           alpha = 1,
           width = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ seqnames, scales = "free_x", nrow = 5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "viridis", guide = "none") +  # Different color scheme to distinguish from first plot
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "grey90"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5,
                               size = 18, margin = margin(t = 5, b = 10)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", color = "black",
                              margin = margin(t = 8, b = 8, l = 5, r = 5)),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey85", color = "grey70",
                                    linewidth = 0.5),
    text = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 22, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.0, "cm"),
    axis.ticks = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Novel RNAs Diversity Across Chromosomes",
    subtitle = "Distribution of novel RNA biotypes per chromosome (log10 scale)",
    x = "Biotype",
    y = "Count (log10 scale)"
  ) +
  geom_text(aes(label = count),
            vjust = -0.5,
            size = 4,
            color = "black")

p22

ggsave("novel_diversity_chr.png", p22,
       width = 20, height = 40, dpi = 300, bg = "white", units = "in")
ggsave("novel_diversity_chr.pdf", p22,
       width = 20, height = 40, bg = "white", units = "in")
```

## 2. Transcript length analysis

Now, we analyze the transcript lengths across novel protein-coding and lncRNAs.

#### Length Distribution by Biotype

We filter for specific biotypes of interest and create boxplots to compare their transcript length distributions.

```{r}
#| label: plot-tx-length-novel
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

median_txlen_novel <- novel_tx %>%
  group_by(biotype) %>%
  summarise(median_length = median(len), .groups = 'drop')

p23 <- ggplot(novel_tx, aes(x = biotype, y = len, fill = factor(biotype))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Transcript length across novel transcripts",
       x = "Biotype",
       y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Biotype\n(median length)") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_txlen_novel$biotype, " (", round(median_txlen_novel$median_length, 2), ")"))
```

We now look deeper into the `protein_coding` and `lncRNA` categories to see how transcript length varies by their respective *transcript* biotypes.

```{r}
#| label: plot-tx-length-detailed-novel
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

# Protein-coding plot
median_pc_novel <- novel_tx %>%
  filter(biotype == "novel protein_coding") %>%
  group_by(classification) %>%
  summarise(median_length_novel = median(len), .groups = 'drop')

p24 <- novel_tx %>%
  filter(biotype == "novel protein_coding") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = biotype, y = len, fill = classification)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Transcript length across novel protein-coding sub-biotypes",
       x = "Biotype", y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Transcript sub-biotypes\n(median length)") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_pc_novel$classification, " (", round(median_pc_novel$median_length_novel, 2), ")"))

# lncRNA plot
median_lnc_novel <- novel_tx %>%
  filter(biotype == "novel lncRNA") %>%
  group_by(classification) %>%
  summarise(median_length_novel = median(len), .groups = 'drop')

p25 <- novel_tx %>%
  filter(biotype == "novel lncRNA") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = biotype, y = len, fill = classification)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Transcript length across novel lncRNA sub-biotypes",
       x = "Biotype", y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Transcript sub-biotypes\n(median length)") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_lnc_novel$classification, " (", round(median_lnc_novel$median_length_novel, 2), ")"))
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-panel5
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 15

panel5 <- plot_grid(p23, p24, p25,nrow = 3,
                    labels="AUTO",
                    label_size = 14,
                    label_y = 1,
                    rel_widths = c(1.5,2,1.5))

ggsave("cowplot_panel_novel_txlen_diversity.png", panel5, width = 8, height = 15, dpi = 600, bg="white", units = "in")

panel5
```

## 3. Exon Structure Analysis

Finally, we compare the exon number and length between novel protein-coding and lncRNAs to see how these features contribute to final transcript length.

#### Exon Number Comparison

Here, we compare the number of exons per transcript for protein-coding genes versus lncRNAs.

```{r}
#| label: plot-exon-number-median_novel
#| fig-width: 5
#| fig-height: 5

median_exnum_novel <- novel_tx %>%
  group_by(biotype) %>%
  summarise(median_exnum = median(num_exons), .groups = 'drop')

p26 <- ggplot(novel_tx, aes(x = biotype, y = num_exons, fill = biotype)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Number of exons across novel transcripts",
       x = "Biotype", y = "Exon number (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Biotype\n(median number)") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_exnum_novel$biotype, " (", round(median_exnum_novel$median_exnum, 2), ")"))

p26

ggsave("novel_exnum_gbiotypes.png", p26, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

Calculate median exon number across specific transcript biotypes

```{r}
#| label: plot-exon-number-median_novel_class
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8

median_exnum_class_novel <- novel_tx %>%
  group_by(biotype, classification) %>%
  summarise(median_exnum = median(num_exons), .groups = 'drop')

p27 <- ggplot(novel_tx, aes(x = classification, y = num_exons, fill = classification)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~biotype, nrow=2) +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)) +
  labs(title = "Number of exons across novel transcripts",
       x = "Classification",
       y = "Exon number (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Classification\n(total median number)") +  # Set legend title
  scale_y_log10() +  # Add log10 scale to y-axis
  scale_fill_discrete(labels = paste0(median_exnum_class_novel$classification, " (", round(median_exnum_class_novel$median_exnum, 2), ")"))  # Set legend labels

p27

ggsave("exnum_class_novel_biotypes.png", p27, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

#### Mono- vs. Multi-exonic Transcript Analysis

We classify transcripts as "mono-exonic" (1 exon) or "multi-exonic" (\>1 exon) and investigate how this structural difference affects transcript counts and final transcript length.

```{r}
#| label: plot-mono-multi-exon-novel
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 8

# Classify transcripts
novel_tx$class_exon <- ifelse(novel_tx$num_exons == 1, "mono-exonic", "multi-exonic")

# Count mono- and multi-exonic transcripts
count_momu_novel <- novel_tx %>%
  group_by(biotype, classification, class_exon) %>%
  summarise(count = n_distinct(qry_id), .groups = 'drop')

p28 <- ggplot(count_momu_novel, aes(x = classification, y = count, fill = classification)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  facet_grid(biotype ~ class_exon, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of mono and multi-exonic novel transcripts",
       x = "Classification", y = "Count (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Classification") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  geom_text(aes(label = count), vjust = -0.5, size=3)

p28

ggsave("momu_tbiotypes_novel.png", p28, width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-mono-multi-exon-2-novel
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 8

# mono and multi exon strctures affect transcript final length

# across tx biotypes

p29 <- ggplot(novel_tx, aes(x = classification, y = len, fill = classification)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_grid(biotype ~ class_exon, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title = "Length of mono and multi-exonic novel transcripts",
    x = "Classification",
    y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
    fill = "Classification"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
  ) +
  scale_y_log10()
p29

ggsave("momu_length_novel_transcripts.png", p29, width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")

```

```{r}
#| label: plot-mono-multi-exon-3-novel
#| fig-width: 5
#| fig-height: 5

# Plot length of mono vs multi-exonic transcripts
p30 <- ggplot(novel_tx, aes(x = biotype, y = len, fill = class_exon)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Mono and multi-exonic novel transcripts` length",
       x = "Biotype", y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Exon classification") +
  scale_y_log10()

p30

ggsave("momu_length_novel_transcripts.png", p30, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

### Exon Number vs. Transcript Length Relationship

A 2D density plot helps visualize the relationship between the number of exons in a transcript and its total length for both protein-coding and lncRNA genes.

```{r}
#| label: plot-density-novel
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

p31 <- ggplot(novel_tx, aes(x = num_exons, y = len)) +
  stat_density2d(aes(fill = after_stat(level)),
                 geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +
  facet_wrap(~biotype, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(x = "Number of exons (log10 scale)",
       y = "Transcript length (log10 scale)",
       title = "Relationship between number of exons and transcript length across novel RNAs")

p31

ggsave("exnum_tlen_novel_transcripts.png", p31, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```

### Individual exon length comparison

Now we gather exon length metadata!

```{r}

exlen_novel_lncRNA <- read.csv(params$novel_lncrna_length)
exlen_novel_pc <- read.csv(params$novel_protein_coding_length)

exlen_novel_lncRNA$biotype <- "novel lncRNA"
exlen_novel_pc$biotype <- "novel protein-coding"

exlen_novel <- rbind(exlen_novel_lncRNA, exlen_novel_pc)

exlen_novel <- merge(
  exlen_novel,
  novel_tx[, c("qry_id", "class_exon")],
  by.x = "transcript_id",
  by.y = "qry_id",
  all.x = TRUE
)
```

Plot

```{r}
p32 <- ggplot(exlen_novel, aes(x = biotype, y = width,
                                   fill = class_exon)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")) +
  labs(title = "Mono and multi-exon individual length across novel protein-coding\nand lncRNA gene biotypes",
       x = "Biotype",
       y = "Exon length (log10 scale)",
       fill = "Exon class") +  # Set legend title
  scale_y_log10() # Add log10 scale to y-axis

p32

ggsave("exlen_momu_novel_biotypes.png", p32, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 600,  bg="white", units = "in")
```
